summary: Apple has pulled Advanced Data Protection (ADP) from the UK rather than comply with a secret government order demanding backdoor access to all iCloud users' encrypted data worldwide. The UK's Investigatory Powers Act made it a criminal offense for Apple to even acknowledge the demand existed, but Apple's conspicuous silence about why they're removing ADP functions as a warrant canary. Gruber explains that iCloud's architecture makes secret compliance technically impossible — new decryption endpoints trigger mandatory notifications to all devices on an account. The post details what happens next for existing UK ADP users and why Apple cannot remotely disable ADP without user consent. Gruber characterizes the UK government's order as simultaneously authoritarian, mathematically ignorant, dangerous, and delusionally imperial in scope.
key_points: The UK demanded not targeted access but blanket capability to view all encrypted iCloud data from every Apple user worldwide, with a gag order preventing Apple from even acknowledging the demand
Apple's statement deliberately offers no explanation for pulling ADP from the UK, functioning as a warrant canary that implicitly confirms the gag order's existence
iCloud's security architecture makes silent compliance technically impossible — adding any new decryption endpoint triggers mandatory notifications to every device on the account
Existing UK ADP users cannot have their protection remotely disabled; Apple will likely issue warnings before eventually cutting off iCloud sync access for non-compliant accounts
The Salt Typhoon attack on US telecoms demonstrates exactly why weakening encryption is dangerous, not theoretical
money_quotes: The bottom line is that the UK government is proceeding like a tyrannical authoritarian state. That's not hyperbole.
Apple is, rightly and righteously, telling them to fuck off.

The breathtaking scope of their order — being able to secretly snoop, without notice that they even have the capability, not only on their own citizens but every Apple user in the entire world — suggests a delusional belief that the British Empire still stands.

It's simultaneously infuriatingly offensive, mathematically ignorant, regarding the nature of end-to-end encryption, dangerous, and laughably naive regarding the UK's actual power and standing in the world.

They're making clear as best they can that they're under a legal gag order that prevents them from even acknowledging that they're under a legal gag order, by not telling us why they're no longer able to offer ADP in the UK.

By definition, end-to-end encryption can have no secret backdoor, so compliance with this order from the UK would, in broad strokes, require Apple to abandon end-to-end encryption — not just for users in the UK but all users in all countries globally.
Apple cannot disable ADP remotely. With a moment's thought, you can realize why they can't: it would defeat the entire purpose.
tone: fiercely opinionated, technically detailed, righteously indignant
key_insight: Apple's refusal to comply with the UK's secret demand for a global encryption backdoor — choosing instead to withdraw the feature entirely — demonstrates both principled resistance to authoritarian overreach and the mathematical impossibility of secretly compromising end-to-end encryption.
